75956.subject:
Warren Buffett Cuts Gates Foundation From Donations For First Time
 Since 2006
75956.body:
Forbes P.M.

–Р–≤—В–Њ—А: Forbes


–Р–≤—В–Њ—А: Forbes

    
    
    –Я—А–Њ—З–Є—В–∞–є—В–µ —Ж—О —Б—В–∞—В—В—О –љ–∞ LinkedIn, —Й–Њ–± –њ—А–Є—Ф–і–љ–∞—В–Є—Б—П –і–Њ –Њ–±–≥–Њ–≤–Њ—А–µ–љ–љ—П
–Я—А–Њ—З–Є—В–∞—В–Є –љ–∞ LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/warren-buffett-cuts-gates-foundation-from-donations-first-gssec?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3Bhf%2FCq%2B7ZSKiBB9t66GTEEQ%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=1Lc42aMYk4JIk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mrl5oa8o~su-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrl5oa8o-su
  
Good evening, Warren Buffett excluded the Gates Foundation from his midyear donations for the first time since 2006 after donating billions last summer. These plans were first reported by the Wall Street Journal in June, which said Buffett was delaying a decision until he could see the results of a review of the Gates FoundationвАЩs ties to disgraced financier Jeffrey Epstein. HereвАЩs what else to know. Thanks for reading! Kris Kornbluth Assistant Editor, Platforms & Curation More Recommended Reads Jeffrey Greenberg/Universal Images Group via Getty Images Taco Bell Investigated In Multistate Cyclosporiasis Outbreak, Report Says As the gastrointestinal illness has sickened thousands across the country, some locations have pulled ingredients as a precaution. Jamel Toppin for Forbes The Backlash Against AI Car Surveillance Company Flock Safety The LAPD said it wouldnвАЩt work with the $7.5 billion startup until it had privacy rules in place related to the data that 138 Flock cameras across the jurisdiction were collecting.
–Я—А–Є—Ф–і–љ–∞—В–Є—Б—П –і–Њ –Њ–±–≥–Њ–≤–Њ—А–µ–љ–љ—П

75951.subject: 25 AI Prompts That Actually Work
75951.body:
The AI Evolution Newsletter

–Р–≤—В–Њ—А: AI Evolution


–Р–≤—В–Њ—А: AI Evolution

    
    
    –Я—А–Њ—З–Є—В–∞–є—В–µ —Ж—О —Б—В–∞—В—В—О –љ–∞ LinkedIn, —Й–Њ–± –њ—А–Є—Ф–і–љ–∞—В–Є—Б—П –і–Њ –Њ–±–≥–Њ–≤–Њ—А–µ–љ–љ—П
–Я—А–Њ—З–Є—В–∞—В–Є –љ–∞ LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/25-ai-prompts-actually-work-ai-evolution-1k0yc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BTKtbwWnASc6JuCVQNXnWlA%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=0R2-Pyb4pyJsk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mrkzzvvc~4c-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrkzzvvc-4c
  
Most people use AI wrong for content. They copy-paste a prompt, get generic output, then spend 30 minutes editing it to not sound like AI. We've been testing prompts for months. Kept the ones that actually work. 25 of them. 5 categories: LinkedIn posts, emails, landing pages, content repurposing, and research. Each one includes the exact prompt, why it works, a real example, and variations to test. No fluff. No filler. Just the prompts we actually use. Grab it free: https://www.chasedimond.com/25-ai-prompts-that-actually-work
–Я—А–Є—Ф–і–љ–∞—В–Є—Б—П –і–Њ –Њ–±–≥–Њ–≤–Њ—А–µ–љ–љ—П
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/25-ai-prompts-actually-work-ai-evolution-1k0yc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BTKtbwWnASc6JuCVQNXnWlA%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=0R2-Pyb4pyJsk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_-null-fz96fz~mrkzzvvc~4c-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrkzzvvc-4c

75950.subject: рЯФЈ The Daily: UK defers crypto tax on DeFi lending
75950.body:
US government moves over $288 million in seized bitcoin, ether to Coinbase Prime: Arkham | US banking groups urge Senate to strengthen stablecoin provisions in Clarity Act | CleanSpark signs $6.6 billion, 20-year data center lease with unnamed global tech firm | European Central Bank taps 36 payment providers for digital euro pilot |

The Daily ( https://www.theblock.co/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Written by James Hunt | Edited by Daniel Kuhn

Happy Tuesday! *Bitcoin* gained after a softer-than-expected U.S. inflation print, with analysts ( https://www.theblock.co/post/408263/soft-print-hard-regime-bitcoin-climbs-64000-june-cpi-falls-largest-monthly-drop-since-2020?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) at *Sygnum* , *21Shares* , and *Wintermute* pointing to easing rate-hike expectations, improving liquidity conditions, and a possible break above $66,000.

In today's newsletter, *HMRC* adopts "no gain, no loss" tax treatment for crypto lending, *U.S. government-linked wallets* move millions of dollars in seized crypto to *Coinbase Prime* , *U.S. banking groups* urge the *Senate* to strengthen the *Clarity Act's* stablecoin provisions, and more.

Meanwhile, decentralized AI project *Nous Research* is reportedly in talks to raise ( https://www.theblock.co/post/408237/decentralized-ai-project-nous-research-in-talks-to-raise-75m-at-1-5b-valuation-report?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) $75 million at a $1.5 billion valuation.

*P.S.* Don't forget to check out The Funding , a biweekly rundown of crypto VC trends. It's a great read вАФ and just like The Daily , it's free to subscribe ( https://www.theblock.co/newsletters?select=The%20Funding&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) !

*THE BIG ONE*

*рЯЗђрЯЗІ UK HMRC adopts 'no gain, no loss' tax treatment for crypto lending, liquidity pools*

( https://www.theblock.co/post/408206/uk-hmrc-adopts-no-gain-no-loss-tax-treatment-for-crypto-lending-liquidity-pools?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

The UK's *HM Revenue & Customs* will treat certain crypto lending and liquidity pool disposals as "no gain, no loss," deferring Capital Gains Tax until users make an economic disposal of the underlying crypto.

* The measure takes effect April 6, 2027, and is expected to affect around 700,000 individuals who use crypto loan and liquidity pool arrangements.
* The rules cover three scenarios: lending arrangements swapped for the same type of crypto; borrowing arrangements, where borrowed crypto is valued at market rate and collateral is disregarded; and liquidity pool entries via automated market makers.
* Any difference

75949.body:
A decade of redefining money вАФ and a personalized look back at your onchain life.

View in browser (https://newsletter.consensys.io/e3t/Ctc/T8+113/czspy04/VVGQ--4zLLPTW30Vv2H1KJjB5W2dmkKk5RtC2BN2dmMxg5m_5PW7Y9pgv6lZ3m1W5KkRMz2c9QyzW5TXV3j3hd4rJW6XL4dN1LHxslW7FFmv-817JlJW4yRVRN2YVz5MW74LFMH8mFbscW1Pc9ZB5nW-mnW7XBmqf7s395kW66cV4M6C3gdpW2DFvTh2j2ty6W6r-plW5LJVTMW3TJ27b2YvnJdW2HBcM86-cmWVW42P9Qn6-xzxJW48szQX4QWVVBW3q-f1q669g7bVTd-Nc8XHmrTW7YTb8c3MYFmXW1NHDZY5j5h13W50whk94fJfwPW8HLpx-2WM-ZYW1FdnhM7XzSP1W4Nz7q-18X-nwW3kZfQD67Hm3sW6nMd1s63MjDmN72y0HhZ424sW37hq5H5fNbVbW1L35M17Y0CJzW6s6ZCh4WWH_dW5Xy-Gr17Bhh0W1v2b848jdc6NW5rDXWb8PFTXmW4Rt_w05Y5Q8VW3WLJY17NNYhHW74Hy9L7dFKSVW27mRSs2khnpxW3xC6329hcbZwW4qzZ4z4G0cbmW7CbsnS5CC1x8W5gPXp73LxfMbW428GkQ6trj_VW73F1mn3c7pW5f6Vpmzs04 )

MetaMask (https://newsletter.consensys.io/e3t/Ctc/T8+113/czspy04/VVGQ--4zLLPTW30Vv2H1KJjB5W2dmkKk5RtC2BN2dmMwH3prCCW6N1vHY6lZ3l5W2VStbT8pXP50W63FKYK3ddMtFW72Qy6t5TrB-gW4R55pc13pwH-W4DHBkj26GYQqW64NrVm3mpDC8N8pcLvyN_XKvW8_Qy6p7GCB6MN3dlR6-_xhsRN5JgyZbXtDrPW5kb0xj1HbwkvW70bm8F4xTXSlN7gZWBywH6lwW3_Lz7H8ycwWdW6tjyzR1YCd4qV-6_J43kw0GnW2LWMyp2bPBQVN6BF3pt_0NyfW3x1RzD2l6-1tW79sYkX93VLTSW39Wts83jN_qWW1WnSTM2-NczPf5jF7bs04 )

A decade of MetaMask

On July 14, 2016, MetaMask went live: one small browser extension, built on the bet that people should be able to hold and use their money directly.вА®

Ten years later: Millions of downloads, used in nearly every country on Earth, trillions of dollars moved. Every transaction signed by someone like you.вА®

We didn't get here alone. Whether you joined in the ICO days, DeFi Summer, the NFT boom, or last week, this milestone is yours too.вА®

So we made a little something for you: a tour of your history onchain, alongside ours. Just open up MetaMask to try it out. (Best experienced on mobile.)

See your life onchain
(https://newsletter.consensys.io/e3t/Ctc/T8+113/czspy04/VVGQ--4zLLPTW30Vv2H1KJjB5W2dmkKk5RtC2BN2dmMwn3gP0xW69sMD-6lZ3m9W4QRvtF6fC7tTVLWPzm466gtvW40sL3Q8VzYV7W3bSCTg4zfzG3W97VglV4cj750W49MW8l7G7Qj5W5fRJDj24RBrWW6FKpst2sJZZ9W293pfv52JdWdW8kqkZK7b-kGZW5wxfRg8sWgb_W394SFb9fz4H5W8LVW_j10qxMgN2tkqnc5HpBzV43vrs8YxpZzW8hP1D_7PwYsnW2XPdT38swQ8HN36wR_D8ltlmW7GNfsB9dn3clW2zQDmZ3DKtc7f7JpZh204 )

75945.subject: Trump has no good options for reopening Hormuz
75945.body:
<link rel=stylesheet href=https://marber-cdn.economist.com/foundations/latest/css/font-face.css>
 The Economist

 
 


 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5Mzd9AAcAAAAAA_p2Zt24UplVtX3zjahkHQdaqO2g5-5JvaIRHMEbYkVsmo9zYvR824BUHtNiik880HeCSxqpcyFcWXtE10K26UofdRieBxkjSL5_VZMMJHTE 

July 14th 2026

 

 
 
 
The Economist Today

 Our best journalism, handpicked each day


 
 
 

 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5Mzd9AAcAAAAAA_p2Zt25BAJa1bPXtogt41zQ_VERPTAqsGAATsSNLXPY1MjrzTwCnxlTBMfSBaw7_S8ogPovETtQ_AF0dKUzL4zbnU8nDKZj_bI9jfXuAIdM 

https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5Mzd9AAcAAAAAA_p2Zt26dK4EIBV4s3YKlnGqGvIy6K6wwzqzWKBM5Oi1oSSxWz3MdagOj4WbIKpNMIjM0QSNZ2f0lij5PKBY1CP2_By0kl2KutyWD9f2LGYc 


 


 
 

Brooke Unger

Senior digital editor



 Missiles and drones are flying in the Middle East and the Strait of Hormuz is closed once again. Oil prices have jumped. But neither Iran nor America is gaining an upper hand from the resumption of conflict and both are suffering (as is the rest of the world). Our analysis shows why the least-bad option for both sides is the truce they are 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5Mzd9AAcAAAAAA_p2Zt27eEs8zVENFUycFhTrvGhDNbGiT4Ye7_xRgiK91A1m3ci06vSAorxDTDn5Yhpq7D4cJk8WoEevQVuk2Ejqeoewm-SaCa1onR0 
shooting to bits .
 


 How do you ensure that artificial intelligence will benefit humanity rather than doom it? Sir Demis Hassabis, a co-founder of Google DeepMind, has an interesting plan. He lays it out 
 
https://click.e.
